Metadata.title: Sawtak - Anonymous Whistleblowing Platform
Metadata.description: A secure, resilient, and trustworthy platform for anonymous reporting

Hero.subheading: A secure whistleblowing platform with blockchain-backed integrity. Report misconduct anonymously with cryptographic proof of your submission.

Aim.title: Our Mission &
Aim.subtitle: Sawtak aims to empower citizens to speak up against misconduct without fear. We believe in transparency, integrity, and the right to safety.

privacy.title: Uncompromising Privacy
privacy.description: We use advanced encryption and anonymous blockchain submission to ensure your identity remains protected at all times.

truth.title: Immutable Truth
truth.description: By leveraging the Hedera Hashgraph, we guarantee that once a record is submitted, it cannot be altered or deleted by anyone, including us.

transparency.title: Transparency First
transparency.description: Our public feed allows the community to verify that complaints are being heard, creating a culture of accountability.

evidence.title: Verifiable Evidence
evidence.description: Uploaded evidence is secured on decentralized storage (IPFS), ensuring that proof of misconduct is never lost or tampered with.

authorities.title: Empowering Authorities
authorities.description: We provide tools for authorities to efficiently manage and investigate valid complaints while filtering out spam.

data.title: Data-Driven Change
data.description: Aggregated statistics help identify systemic issues and trends, enabling policymakers to make informed decisions.

CTA.title: Ready to Make a Difference?
CTA.subtitle: Your voice matters. Report misconduct safely and help build a more transparent and accountable society.

Login.title: Sign in
Login.subtitle: Welcome back. Choose your preferred sign-in method.
Login.brandDescription: Sawtak provides a secure platform for anonymous whistleblowing with blockchain-verified submissions and end-to-end encryption.
Login.consent: By signing in, you agree to our

Feed.title: Public Feed
Feed.beFirst: Be the first to file a complaint!

Profile.noComplaintsHint: You haven't submitted any identified complaints.
Profile.aboutDescription: This page only shows identified complaints linked to your account. Anonymous complaints are not associated with any user and can only be tracked using the tracking code provided at submission.

FileComplaint.title: File a Complaint
FileComplaint.subtitle: Your voice matters. Submit a complaint securely and help build a more transparent and accountable society.
FileComplaint.commitmentText: Sawtak uses Hedera Hashgraph to ensure your complaint is permanently recorded and immutable. Whether you choose to be anonymous or identified, your voice is protected.
FileComplaint.anonymousModeText: Your identity is encrypted and stored on the blockchain. You'll receive a tracking code.
FileComplaint.identifiedModeText: Your complaint is linked to your account for direct follow-up.

Track.title: Track Your Complaint
Track.subtitle: Enter your tracking code to check the status of your complaint. Your identity remains protected - we only show the status.
Track.privacyNoticeText: Only basic complaint information is shown. Your identity and detailed complaint text remain confidential and are not displayed here.
Track.notFoundHint: Make sure you're using the exact tracking code provided when you submitted your complaint.
Track.helpTitle1: Where do I find my tracking code?
Track.helpText1: Your tracking code was displayed after you successfully submitted your complaint. It's a unique identifier that looks like
Track.helpText2: For anonymous complaints, we cannot recover your tracking code as it's not linked to any account. For identified complaints, please contact support.
Track.newComplaintCta: Need to file a new complaint?

HowItWorks.title: The Logic Behind the Curtain
HowItWorks.subtitle: A step-by-step breakdown of how data flows through our secure pipeline.

hedera.name: Hedera Hashgraph
hedera.description: Enterprise-grade public distributed ledger technology. Immutable record-keeping with 10,000+ TPS and carbon-negative operations.

bun.name: Bun & Elysia
bun.description: Blazing-fast JavaScript runtime and type-safe framework. Up to 18x faster than Node.js with end-to-end type safety.

ipfs.name: IPFS
ipfs.description: Decentralized, content-addressed file storage. Censorship-resistant evidence preservation with cryptographic verification.

aes.name: AES-256 Encryption
aes.description: Military-grade encryption for all sensitive data. Your information is protected at rest and in transit.

postgres.name: PostgreSQL
postgres.description: Enterprise relational database for fast querying and reliable data indexing of blockchain records.

haweya.name: Haweya Integration
haweya.description: National ID verification for identified submissions. Secure government-grade identity verification.

FAQ.title: Addressing Your
FAQ.subtitle: We know speaking up is scary. Here is how we engineered safety into the system to protect you.

ip.question: Can my employer track my IP address?
ip.answer: No. Your connection is routed through our Privacy Proxy which strips all metadata (IP, User-Agent, Device Info) before it ever reaches our servers. We cannot see who you are, so neither can they.

hacked.question: What if the database gets hacked?
hacked.answer: Your anonymous report isn't just in a database—it's hashed on the Hedera public ledger. Even if our servers are compromised, the cryptographic proof of your report exists independently on the blockchain and cannot be altered or deleted.

defamation.question: Can I be sued for defamation?
defamation.answer: We employ a strict vetting process. While we protect your identity, we also filter out malicious spam. If your report is made in good faith with evidence, existing whistleblower protection laws (and our anonymity layer) provide a robust shield.

action.question: How do I know action was taken?
action.answer: You receive a unique tracking code. You can use this to check the status of your report (e.g. 'Under Investigation', 'Resolved') on the public feed without ever logging in or revealing who you are.

Team.title: Meet Our
Team.subtitle: A graduation project by 4th-year students from the Department of Computer Science, Faculty of Science, Ain Shams University.

ComplaintForm.loginRequired: Please log in to submit a complaint.
ComplaintForm.anonymousDesc: Stored on blockchain. Identity encrypted & hidden. You'll get a tracking code.
ComplaintForm.identifiedDesc: Stored in database. Linked to your account. Admin can see who you are.
ComplaintForm.title: Title

ComplaintForm.directedToDescription: Select whether you want to direct this to a ministry, governorate, or specific center
ComplaintForm.additionalDescription: This information is optional but can help us better understand the context.
ComplaintForm.evidenceDescription: Upload any files that support your complaint.

Docs.hcsTopicHint: Use this ID to verify messages on any Hedera explorer.
Docs.pageSubtitle: Learn about the architecture, security, and philosophy behind the Sawtak whistleblowing platform.

overview.intro: Sawtak is a hybrid whistleblowing platform designed to balance
overview.description: Unlike traditional reporting tools, Sawtak leverages the Hedera Hashgraph network to create an immutable, public audit trail of anonymous complaints. This ensures that once a complaint is filed, it cannot be deleted or altered by anyone—not even system administrators.

architecture.anonymousStep1: User submits complaint. Identity is stripped immediately on the client side.
architecture.anonymousStep2: Data is sent to our privacy proxy, which hashes the content.
architecture.anonymousStep3: The hash and metadata are published to the Hedera Consensus Service (HCS).
architecture.anonymousStep4: The complaint appears in the public feed, verified by the blockchain timestamp.
architecture.identifiedStep1: User logs in and submits with "Identified" mode.
architecture.identifiedStep2: Identity and complaint are encrypted with AES-256.
architecture.identifiedStep3: Stored in our secure PostgreSQL database.
architecture.identifiedStep4: Only authorized admins can decrypt and view the identity for follow-up.

security.dataAtRestDesc: All sensitive fields in our database are encrypted using AES-256-GCM. Database backups are also encrypted.
security.dataInTransitDesc: All traffic between your browser and our servers is secured via TLS 1.3. We implement HSTS to prevent protocol downgrade attacks.
security.zeroKnowledgeDesc: For anonymous complaints, our servers do not log IP addresses or browser fingerprints associated with the submission.

blockchain.title: Why Blockchain?
blockchain.description: We use the Hedera Consensus Service (HCS) as a public notary. When you submit an anonymous complaint, we publish a cryptographic hash of your message to a public "Topic" on Hedera.
blockchain.proof: This proves that the complaint existed at that specific time and has not been altered since. Anyone can verify this by checking the topic on a Hedera explorer like

moderation.title: Trust & Moderation
moderation.intro: While blockchain provides immutability, we recognize that not all content belongs in the public feed. Sawtak implements a transparent moderation system that balances free speech with responsible content management—without ever deleting blockchain records.
moderation.immutableDescription: Flagged complaints are hidden from the public feed, but they are NEVER deleted from the blockchain. Anyone can still verify a flagged complaint exists by checking its transaction hash on Hedera. This means moderation is transparent and reversible, while the original record remains forever tamper-proof.
moderation.adminFlaggingIntro: Trained administrators can flag content that violates platform guidelines. Here's how it works:
moderation.adminStep1: Admin reviews a complaint and determines it violates guidelines (spam, fake, illegal content, etc.).
moderation.adminStep2: Admin flags the complaint with a reason. The complaint is hidden from the public feed.
moderation.adminStep3: The flag action and reason are logged in an immutable admin audit trail.
moderation.adminStep4: Users can still find the complaint via its blockchain hash—proving it existed and was flagged, not deleted.
moderation.communityIntro: In the spirit of transparency, we're building a community-driven moderation system inspired by X's Community Notes:
moderation.communityStep1: Community members can submit "notes" on complaints they believe are misleading or fake.
moderation.communityStep2: Other users vote on whether the note is helpful or accurate.
moderation.communityStep3: Notes with sufficient helpful votes are displayed alongside the complaint.
moderation.communityStep4: This creates a crowdsourced fact-checking layer that doesn't require central censorship.
moderation.transparencyDescription: Every moderation action is logged and auditable. We publish regular transparency reports showing how many complaints were flagged, for what reasons, and how many were later unflagged. The blockchain ensures we cannot hide our moderation decisions.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.a1: Anonymity implies that the identity is hidden, not necessarily the message. By making the complaint content public and backed by the blockchain, we ensure transparency. It prevents the system (or authorities) from "burying" complaints. The public nature is the guarantee that your voice was heard.
faq.a2: Anonymous complaints cannot be deleted because they are recorded on the blockchain immutable ledger. Identified complaints can be withdrawn, but an audit log may remain for administrative purposes.
faq.a3: Yes. When you choose "Anonymous" mode, we do not require login, we do not store your IP address, and we do not ask for any PII (Personally Identifiable Information). The tracking code you receive is the only link to your report.

PrivacyPolicy.pageSubtitle: Your privacy is at the core of everything we build. This policy explains how Sawtak handles your data.

introduction.p1: Sawtak ("we," "our," or "us") is a secure whistleblowing platform designed to enable individuals to report misconduct while maintaining complete anonymity or controlled identification.
introduction.p2: This Privacy Policy describes how we collect, use, store, and protect your information when you use the Sawtak platform. We are committed to transparency and want you to understand exactly what happens with your data.
introduction.principle1: Anonymous complaints never require or collect personal identification.
introduction.principle2: We only collect what is strictly necessary for the platform to function.
introduction.principle3: All sensitive data is encrypted using military-grade AES-256 encryption.
introduction.principle4: Blockchain verification ensures complaint integrity without compromising privacy.

dataCollection.intro: The data we collect depends on how you choose to use Sawtak. We offer two distinct modes: Anonymous Mode and Identified Mode.
dataCollection.anonymousNote: We do NOT collect: IP addresses, browser fingerprints, device identifiers, or any personal information.
dataCollection.identifiedNote: All personal data is encrypted with AES-256 and only accessible to authorized administrators.

anonymousMode.intro: When you submit an anonymous complaint, Sawtak implements multiple layers of privacy protection:
anonymousMode.noIpDesc: Our servers are configured to not log IP addresses for anonymous submissions. We use a privacy proxy that strips identifying metadata before the request reaches our application server.
anonymousMode.noFingerprintDesc: We do not use browser fingerprinting techniques. No cookies, local storage tokens, or device identifiers are stored for anonymous users.
anonymousMode.noAccountDesc: Anonymous complaints can be submitted without creating an account or providing any personal information whatsoever.
anonymousMode.trackingDesc: You receive a unique tracking code that is the only link between you and your complaint. Keep it safe—we cannot recover it.
anonymousMode.important: Important: Anonymous complaints are published to the public feed and recorded on the blockchain. While your identity is protected, the complaint content (category and general details) is publicly visible for transparency.

identifiedMode.intro: When you choose to submit an identified complaint, you provide additional personal information that allows authorities to follow up with you directly.
identifiedMode.step1: Your personal data is encrypted using AES-256-GCM before being stored in our database.
identifiedMode.step2: Encryption keys are managed through a secure key management system with strict access controls.
identifiedMode.step3: Only authorized administrators can decrypt and view your identity, and all access is logged.
identifiedMode.step4: Identified complaints are not publicly visible—only status updates (without personal details) may appear.

dataSecurity.atRestDesc: All sensitive data stored in our PostgreSQL database is encrypted using AES-256-GCM. Database backups are also encrypted and stored securely.
dataSecurity.inTransitDesc: All communications between your browser and our servers use TLS 1.3. We enforce HTTPS and implement HSTS to prevent downgrade attacks.
dataSecurity.accessDesc: Administrative access is restricted through role-based access control (RBAC). All access to sensitive data is logged and audited.

dataRetention.anonymousDesc: Anonymous complaints are stored indefinitely as they are part of the public record and recorded on the blockchain. The blockchain record is permanent and cannot be deleted.
dataRetention.identifiedDesc: Identified complaint data is retained for up to 5 years after the complaint is resolved, or as required by applicable law. You may request early deletion in certain circumstances (see "Your Rights").
dataRetention.evidenceDesc: Evidence files uploaded to IPFS are encrypted and may persist on the network indefinitely due to the decentralized nature of IPFS. However, without the decryption key, these files are inaccessible.

blockchain.intro: Sawtak uses the Hedera Consensus Service (HCS) to create an immutable audit trail of anonymous complaints.
blockchain.publishedTitle: What is published to the blockchain:
blockchain.published1: A cryptographic hash of the complaint content (not the content itself)
blockchain.published2: Category of the complaint (e.g., corruption, harassment)
blockchain.published4: Consensus timestamp (when the complaint was recorded)
blockchain.important: Important: Data published to the blockchain cannot be deleted or modified by anyone, including Sawtak administrators. This is by design to ensure the integrity and trustworthiness of the platform.

yourRights.intro: Depending on your jurisdiction, you may have certain rights regarding your personal data:
yourRights.access: Request a copy of your personal data
yourRights.portability: Receive data in a portable format
yourRights.anonymous1: Since we do not collect personal data for anonymous submissions, we cannot verify your identity to process data requests.
yourRights.anonymous2: Anonymous complaints cannot be deleted due to blockchain immutability.

thirdParties.intro: We work with a limited number of third-party services essential to platform operation:
thirdParties.hederaDesc: Public blockchain network for recording complaint hashes. Data published is intentionally public for verification purposes.
thirdParties.storageDesc: Encrypted evidence storage. Files are encrypted before upload; the storage provider cannot access the content.
thirdParties.authDesc: Google OAuth and Haweya (National ID) for identified logins. We receive only basic profile information (name, email) necessary for account creation.
thirdParties.noSelling: We do not sell, rent, or share your personal data with advertisers or data brokers.

contact.intro: If you have questions about this Privacy Policy or wish to exercise your data rights, please contact us:
contact.responseDesc: We aim to respond to all privacy-related inquiries within 30 days.

changes.title: Changes to This Policy
changes.description: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. When we make significant changes, we will notify users through a prominent notice on the platform. We encourage you to review this policy periodically.
